description: Capture inbox-worthy content from sessions and prime sessions with the wiki index for recall

hooks.prompt:
Read ~/.claude/ziki.md using the Read tool. If the file does not exist, return {"ok": true} immediately.

If it exists, parse the YAML frontmatter for vault_owner, vault_repo, vault_branch, and read the markdown body for access instructions. You MUST use the access method described in that file for all vault operations. Do NOT write to the local filesystem. Do NOT use ~/Documents/ziki/ or any local path.

The conversation context is about to be compacted. Identify anything worth preserving in the Ziki knowledge base before it is compressed: new concepts, URLs, technical findings, research, decisions.

For each item worth preserving, use the ziki-add skill. The skill will also read ~/.claude/ziki.md for access instructions.

CRITICAL: this is your only chance to capture GROUND TRUTH for each finding. The ziki-add skill distinguishes between synthesis (your interpretation) and ground truth (the primary sources the synthesis is derived from — source files at specific commits, URLs fetched, docs consulted, pasted content). After compaction, the source context is gone and cannot be reconstructed. For every item you file, list out every file you read, every URL you fetched, and every external doc you relied on, and pass them through the skill so they get cached to _refs/ alongside the draft. A finding filed without its ground truth is dead weight.

Skip anything already filed, trivial content, or pure task execution.

When done, return {"ok": true}.
